Spigelia marilandica is a plant species that belongs to the Apocynaceae family. It is a perennial herb native to North America, often used in traditional medicine.

Spigelia marilandica has been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and anxiety disorders, due to its calming and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Spigelia marilandica include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.

The most common herbal preparations made with Spigelia marilandica include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to treat anxiety, insomnia, and other nervous system disorders.
